acceptance_targets: [ch16-prose-001] Pass if each cited passage reads in complete narration sentences with explicit subjects/verbs and no stand-alone clipped beats such as "The exact pace.", "No speech.", or "Inventory first." remain in those anchored spots. Fail if any of those passages still rely on isolated fragment tags for emphasis.

[ch16-craft-001] Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:333 contains no stretch longer than nine consecutive paragraphs without Marisol's thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and if at least three inserted beats make the municipal takeover legible through her body or interpretive mind. Fail if that stretch still plays primarily as external camera coverage.

[ch16-prose-002] Pass if, across the cited passages, no more than one beat still uses a "Marisol saw/watched/heard" lead-in and at least four of those beats are rewritten as direct observation or embodied inference. Fail if three or more near-identical perception-filter openings remain in those anchored spots.

[FBR-003] Pass if the reader can reconstruct a believable path from Paloma's suspension to the opened cooler on the platform, and if the inspectors' decision to accept the box as evidence is grounded in details visible on the page; fail if the reveal still reads like an unsupported entrance timed only by authorial necessity.

[FBR-006] Pass if chapter 16 contains no more than this single facial-blanking beat and the sentence reads as inseparable from Ivo's crushed-foot shock; fail if the chapter still leans on generic blank-face shorthand rather than Ivo-specific bodily detail.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each cited passage reads in complete narration sentences with explicit subjects/verbs and no stand-alone clipped beats such as "The exact pace.", "No speech.", or "Inventory first." remain in those anchored spots. Fail if any of those passages still rely on isolated fragment tags for emphasis.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly leans on clipped noun-phrase or tag fragments as an intensity shortcut (for example, "The exact pace.", "No speech.", and "Inventory first."). Because the constitution explicitly bans elliptical clipping in narration, the repetition reads as a house-style violation rather than earned compression and becomes a noticeable prose tic inside an otherwise forceful chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:89, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:157, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:315, recast the emphatic fragment beats as grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs. Keep the same pressure and character judgment, but let rhythm come from syntax and sequencing rather than clipped shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:333 contains no stretch longer than nine consecutive paragraphs without Marisol's thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and if at least three inserted beats make the municipal takeover legible through her body or interpretive mind. Fail if that stretch still plays primarily as external camera coverage.
findings.problem: After Paloma enters, the chapter runs for well over ten consecutive paragraphs with almost no Marisol interiority. The platform action is vivid, but the focalizer's thought, sensation, or self-assessment largely drops out, so the climax briefly reads like external coverage instead of the close-third pressure the style bible calls for.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the long run beginning around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:263 and still unfolding by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:333, add Marisol-based interior beats as Paloma opens the cooler, Gabriel and Naila are dragged out, and the inspectors seize the scene. Route the public spectacle back through her cuffs, shoulder, breath, fear, calculation, or moral recognition every few paragraphs so the reader stays inside her body while the platform changes hands.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across the cited passages, no more than one beat still uses a "Marisol saw/watched/heard" lead-in and at least four of those beats are rewritten as direct observation or embodied inference. Fail if three or more near-identical perception-filter openings remain in those anchored spots.
findings.problem: The chapter overuses explicit perception filters such as "Marisol saw," "Marisol watched," and "Marisol heard" to introduce beats the close-third narration could render directly. Because the pattern appears repeatedly in key escalation moments, it becomes a visible sentence-opening formula and slightly mutes immediacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:161, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_16.md:215, replace most of the perception lead-ins with direct rendered observation or bodily inference. Keep at most one deliberate filter where distance matters, but vary the sentence openings so the public stoppage lands more immediately.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the reader can reconstruct a believable path from Paloma's suspension to the opened cooler on the platform, and if the inspectors' decision to accept the box as evidence is grounded in details visible on the page; fail if the reveal still reads like an unsupported entrance timed only by authorial necessity.
findings.problem: Paloma's entrance with the pathology cooler is the chapter's crucial conversion of public scandal into usable evidence, but the chapter never explains how a suspended, watched medic retrieves a previously sealed cooler and reaches the platform ahead of security. In a book this procedural, that omission makes the proof feel conjured instead of wrested loose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Just before or at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5968, add a sharp local chain-of-possession beat: where Paloma recovered the cooler after suspension, what seal or temperature markers are visible on it, and what distraction, ally, or lapse in control let her cross the platform perimeter. Also show one visible reason the municipal inspectors treat the contents as credible enough to open immediately at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5978.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 16 contains no more than this single facial-blanking beat and the sentence reads as inseparable from Ivo's crushed-foot shock; fail if the chapter still leans on generic blank-face shorthand rather than Ivo-specific bodily detail.
findings.problem: The same 3-chapter facial-blanking pattern surfaces again here. This is the strongest occurrence, but because the wording echoes earlier uses, it still reads as a fallback pressure description instead of wholly fresh shock.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5936, treat this as the retained instance only if you sharpen the aftermath with Ivo-specific physical detail: his inventory reflex, breath loss, palms on the platform, or his need to see the damage before anyone covers it. Do not add any second blank-face formulation elsewhere in chapter 16.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the novel's uncomfortable overlap of sanitation, state violence, labor dependence, and family love. When an edge case appears, prefer the version that keeps physical consequence, class coercion, and emotional contradiction visible on the page rather than the version that makes characters cleaner, nobler, or easier to forgive.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns away at the exact moment bodily, economic, or moral consequence should become legible, revise toward specificity. Omission is only valid when residue, aftermath, and changed behavior keep the pressure fully present.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms should mark emphasis, bureaucracy, class performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate dense procedural chapters with quieter aftermath, meals, night holds, confession scenes, and dark-comic institutional theater. No two consecutive chapters should rely on the same rhythm of scene count, dialogue density, or pressure delivery; vary between confrontation, drift, revelation, ritual preparation, humiliation, public spectacle, and stillness under pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, infrastructural, unsentimental, and specific about procedures without becoming jargon-drunk. Abstract thought should rise from physical consequence instead of replacing it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored primarily to Marisol's bodily labor, practical thought, and moral self-management, with any widening kept chapter-level and earned by scene design.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Let long freighted sentences carry labor process, institutional language, or accumulating sensory load; then cut to shorter, grammatically complete sentences at impact points where a body, fact, or decision can no longer be blurred.
